channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Why Daily Moderate Activity, Not Step Count, Matters Most for Health
item.description: For years, the widely cited goal of walking 10,000 steps per day has been considered a standard for maintaining health. However, this number originated from a 1960s Japanese pedometer rather than scie...

item.title: Experiencing Sustainable Living in Remote Coastal Accommodations
item.description: This article explores the unique experience of staying in off-grid accommodations along the northern coast, particularly in Haida Gwaii. It emphasizes the opportunity for travelers to disconnect from ...

item.title: Shifaa Hospital launches new respiratory department to fight deadly lung diseases
item.description: Dar es Salaam residents have been advised to adopt a culture of regular testing for respiratory illnesses, as these conditions continue to cause significant deaths in Tanzania. Dr Mwanaada Kilima, a r...

item.title: Ramaphosa proposes permanent national transport council to boost logistics and economic growth
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has proposed the creation of a permanent national transport council to streamline South Africa's logistics and transport systems. Speaking at the first national transport con...

item.title: Guardiola Confident Man City Can Overturn 3-0 Deficit Against Real Madrid
item.description: Pep Guardiola, the Man City gaffer, has told his boys that they can turn things around against Real Madrid in the Champions League second leg. After losing 3-0 in the first leg at Santiago Bernabéu, C...

item.title: Suspect in Mayor Ampatuan RPG attack surrenders and gives testimony
item.description: A key lookout involved in the fourth assassination attempt against Mayor Akmad Mitra Ampatuan Sr. of Shariff Aguak, Maguindanao del Sur, voluntarily surrendered to the National Bureau of Investigation...

item.title: African seas rising faster than global average due to warming and ice melt
item.description: For over thirty years, satellites don dey monitor ocean heights sharply, and dem don show say sea levels around Africa don rise by about 11.26cm since 1993. This one dey happen mainly because water de...

item.title: Jemima requests urgent permission for Imran Khan’s sons to visit their father in jail
item.description: Jemima Goldsmith, ex-wife of PTI founder Imran Khan, has directly appealed to Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif to allow her two sons, Kasim and Suleiman Khan, to visit their father in Adiala jail, Rawalp...

item.title: Meningitis outbreak at University of Kent and local school leads to precautionary antibiotic distribution
item.description: A meningitis outbreak has affected the Canterbury area in Kent, resulting in the deaths of two young people: a University of Kent student and a Year 13 pupil from Queen Elizabeth’s Grammar School in F...

item.title: Extreme Heat's Impact on Physical and Mental Health in Vulnerable Communities in Pakistan and Kenya
item.description: Extreme heatwaves in places like Karachi in Pakistan and Nairobi in Kenya are not just about high temperatures but are becoming a serious public health crisis that affects both the body and mind. When...

item.title: Pakistan Conducts Targeted Strikes on Taliban Military Installations in Kabul and Nangarhar
item.description: On Monday night, Pakistani forces carried out precise military strikes on the Afghan Taliban’s installations in Kabul and Nangarhar, according to security sources. This action is part of Operation Gha...

item.title: Tanzania launches initiative to train more AI and Data Science professionals
item.description: The Government of Tanzania has unveiled a national drive to expand the number of professionals in Information and Communication Technology (ICT), Artificial Intelligence (AI), and Data Science. The in...

item.title: KNCHR Clarifies Objectives of Reparations for Protest Victims in Kenya
item.description: The Kenya National Commission on Human Rights (KNCHR) has clarified that the upcoming reparations for victims of protest-related violence are aimed at achieving justice rather than just financial payo...

item.title: Federal Constabulary to be deployed in Balochistan to strengthen security and law enforcement
item.description: The federal and provincial governments of Pakistan have agreed on a phased deployment of the Federal Constabulary in Balochistan to reinforce law and order in the province. The decision was made in a ...

item.title: Around 200 U.S. Troops Injured as Iran Conflict Enters Third Week
item.description: The number of United States military personnel wounded in the ongoing conflict with Iran has reached approximately 200, according to the U.S. Central Command. Most of the injured troops sustained mino...

item.title: Philippines Reaffirms Sovereignty Over Scarborough Shoal Amid China Dispute
item.description: The Philippines has strongly rejected claims by China over Bajo de Masinloc, also known as Scarborough Shoal, in the South China Sea. The Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) emphasized that Philippine...

item.title: Calgary, Edmonton, and Prague Selected as Hosts for 2028 World Cup of Hockey
item.description: The 2028 World Cup of Hockey will be jointly hosted by Calgary, Edmonton, and Prague, marking a significant international collaboration in the world of ice hockey. This announcement highlights Canada’...

item.title: How Formula 1’s 2026 Sustainability Rules Are Shaping Race Strategy
item.description: The 2026 Formula 1 season has introduced substantial rule changes aimed at enhancing sustainability and overtaking. At the Australian Grand Prix, the number of on-track overtakes nearly tripled from t...

item.title: Supriya Sule confirms NCP (SP) will back Sunetra Pawar in Baramati bypoll
item.description: Nationalist Congress Party (Sharad Pawar faction) working president Supriya Sule announced on Monday that her party would not field a candidate against Maharashtra Deputy Chief Minister Sunetra Pawar ...

item.title: Divergence Between Paper and Physical Oil Prices Amid Middle East Conflict Expectations
item.description: The article examines the current state of the global oil market, highlighting a significant divergence between futures prices traded on exchanges and actual physical cargo prices. Oil, like stocks, is...
